title: DPI Calculator - Dots Per Inch for Screens & Print
description: Free DPI calculator. Calculate screen PPI from resolution and size, or find required resolution for target DPI. Essential for designers.
keywords: DPI calculator, PPI calculator, screen resolution DPI, dots per inch, pixels per inch calculator
intro: Calculate DPI (dots per inch) or PPI (pixels per inch) from screen resolution and physical size. Find the right resolution for your needs.

faq.q: What is the difference between DPI and PPI?
faq.a: PPI (pixels per inch) refers to screen display density. DPI (dots per inch) refers to print resolution. They're often used interchangeably.

faq.q: What DPI is good for printing?
faq.a: 300 DPI is the standard for high-quality printing. 150 DPI is acceptable for large format prints viewed from a distance.

faq.q: How do I calculate my screen's PPI?
faq.a: PPI = √(width² + height²) / diagonal size in inches. For example, a 24" 1920×1080 monitor has about 92 PPI.
